title: Number Systems: Binary and Hexadecimal
summary: Convert between decimal, binary, and hexadecimal, and understand why computers use these systems.
content_html:
Computers use positional number systems. In decimal (base-10), each digit position represents a power of 10. In binary (base-2), each position represents a power of 2; in hexadecimal (base-16), a power of 16.

Binary Numbers

Binary uses only 0 and 1. To convert binary to decimal, multiply each bit by its positional power of 2 and sum:

01001110₂ = 0×2⁷ + 1×2⁶ + 0×2⁵ + 0×2⁴ + 1×2³ + 1×2² + 1×2¹ + 0×2⁰
           = 64 + 8 + 4 + 2 = 78₁₀

Hexadecimal Numbers

Hex uses digits 0–9 and A–F (A=10, B=11, C=12, D=13, E=14, F=15). Each hex digit maps exactly to 4 binary bits:

4E₁₆ = 0100 1110₂ = 78₁₀
(4 = 0100, E = 1110)

Converting Decimal to Binary

Repeatedly divide by 2 and record remainders (bottom-up):
78 ÷ 2 = 39 R 0
39 ÷ 2 = 19 R 1
19 ÷ 2 = 9 R 1
9 ÷ 2 = 4 R 1
4 ÷ 2 = 2 R 0
2 ÷ 2 = 1 R 0
1 ÷ 2 = 0 R 1
Result: 1001110₂

Common Prefixes

  • Binary: 0b01110 or %01110
  • Hexadecimal: 0x1E or 1Eh
